hero_subtitle: SFTY helps compliance teams connect alerts, provider data and reviews into audit-ready evidence packages to support suspicious activity reporting workflows with full traceability.

problem_title: Evidence reconstruction slows suspicious activity review
problem_description: Compliance teams struggle to assemble complete and audit-ready SAR evidence packages when provider outputs and case data remain separated across multiple systems. This fragmentation increases manual workload and weakens traceability between alerts, decisions and regulatory reporting requirements.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY links provider outputs, alerts, decisions and regulatory data into unified SAR evidence packages so compliance teams can efficiently assemble, trace and audit suspicious activity reports.
workflow_title: From alerts to SAR evidence packages
workflow_description: SFTY organizes alerts and provider outputs into structured cases, preserving review decisions and compiling evidence to support SAR-style compliance reporting workflows.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit

why_sfty_description: SFTY connects AML alerts, provider outputs and case decisions into a structured workflow that preserves evidence and supports SAR drafting and audit readiness.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools

why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view

authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for AML compliance teams?
faq_1_answer: SFTY connects provider outputs into structured workflows, enabling AML teams to manage alerts, cases, decision logs, and reporting workflows efficiently within a connected compliance operations layer.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY support SAR evidence packages?
faq_2_answer: SFTY aggregates data from KYC, KYT, Travel Rule, and screening providers to compile comprehensive SAR evidence packages linked to relevant cases and internal reports.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY maintains detailed case histories, decision logs, evidence attachments, and audit trails to support inspection readiness and defensible regulatory reviews.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing compliance providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY sits above KYC, KYT, screening, and Travel Rule providers, connecting their outputs into harmonized workflows without altering existing systems.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace our current AML providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace AML providers but enhances their outputs by structuring reviews, preserving evidence, and orchestrating case management within one operational layer.
final_title: Structure SAR evidence packages with SFTY
final_description: Review how SFTY connects alerts, provider outputs and case records into audit-ready evidence for suspicious activity reporting workflows.
